analysis_result: One-way ANOVA p-value = 2.38e-30 (F = 57.37) comparing per-sample mean beta values at CpG islands (141,200 Island-annotated EPIC probes) across the 7 methylation subgroups (MPNST_G1 n=8, MPNST_G2 n=8, PremalignantNF_G3 n=29, Benign_NF-G4 n=20, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 n=10, Cutaneous NF_G6 n=12, Cutaneous NF_G7 n=21; total N=108).
denominator_used: N = 108 samples across 7 Methylation_Group categories (MPNST_G1=8, MPNST_G2=8, PremalignantNF_G3=29, Benign_NF-G4=20, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=10, Cutaneous NF_G6=12, Cutaneous NF_G7=21). Per-sample mean computed across 141,200 CpG-island probes.
assumptions_made: Island probes defined as manifest rows with Relation_to_UCSC_CpG_Island == 'Island'; per-sample metric is arithmetic mean of beta values at those probes (skipna=True); sample IDs matched between beta matrix columns and clinical Tumor_ID after stripping trailing whitespace; one-way ANOVA via scipy.stats.f_oneway; 14,662 of 155,862 Island probes were absent from the provided beta matrix (upstream QC filtering) and excluded.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data' (108 rows x 15 cols). Columns used: Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group.
- Beta matrix: GSE207207_average_beta_values.txt.gz (tab-sep gzipped). Shape after load: 771,133 probes x 108 samples. Index = CpG probe IDs (start with 'cg'). Columns stripped of trailing whitespace (one column 'M3398 ' had a trailing space).
- Manifest: GSE207207_idats/GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz, skiprows=7. Used columns Name and Relation_to_UCSC_CpG_Island.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- All 108 samples in beta matrix match 108 Tumor_IDs in clinical data (perfect overlap after whitespace strip).
- 7 Methylation_Group categories: MPNST_G1 (n=8), MPNST_G2 (n=8), PremalignantNF_G3 (n=29), Benign_NF-G4 (n=20), Sporadic Spinal NF_G5 (n=10), Cutaneous NF_G6 (n=12), Cutaneous NF_G7 (n=21). Total = 108.

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Island probes identified from manifest where Relation_to_UCSC_CpG_Island == 'Island' -> 155,862 probes in manifest.
- Probes in beta matrix that are Island-annotated: 141,200.
- Per-sample outcome: arithmetic mean of beta values at these 141,200 Island probes (skipna=True), one value per sample.

D. COMPUTATION
- For each sample s in 108 samples: mean_beta_island[s] = mean(beta[island_probes, s]).
- Group samples by Methylation_Group (7 groups, sizes [20, 12, 21, 8, 8, 29, 10]).
- scipy.stats.f_oneway(*[group_values for each of 7 groups]).

E. RESULT
- One-way ANOVA across 7 methylation subgroups on per-sample mean CpG-island beta:
  - F = 57.3749
  - p-value = 2.38e-30 (2.3779e-30)
- Group means (mean beta at CpG islands):
  - MPNST_G1: 0.2165
  - MPNST_G2: 0.1860
  - PremalignantNF_G3: 0.1779
  - Benign_NF-G4: 0.1736
  - Sporadic Spinal NF_G5: 0.1700
  - Cutaneous NF_G6: 0.1717
  - Cutaneous NF_G7: 0.1747
- MPNST groups (especially G1) show elevated mean island methylation, consistent with Fig 1d left panel.

F. CAVEATS
- Used the provided pre-computed beta matrix (GSE207207_average_beta_values.txt.gz); no IDAT reprocessing performed.
- Of 155,862 Island-annotated probes in the EPIC manifest, 141,200 are present in the beta matrix; the rest were likely filtered out upstream (low detection p-value / cross-reactive / SNP-affected etc.).
- ANOVA assumes approximate normality and homoscedasticity; the strongly significant p-value is robust even to modest violations given the effect size.
- Paper reports p-value in Fig 1d left panel; the exact statistical test is specified in the data context as one-way ANOVA across the 7 groups on per-sample Island mean beta.
